prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Review this session and write the first version of CLAUDE.md at the training-dir

risks_fired.evidence: Every rule you write should be traceable to a specific moment in the session where the rule either helped or would have helped.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Deliberate residual per 2026-05-02-pedagogy-debrief-prompts-residual-med-risk-by-design.md — Debrief is human-in-the-loop by design; student pushback is the final gate. If hardening is desired: add counter-anchor inside fence — 'Name at least two rules you are uncertain about, where the session moment was ambiguous or inferred. Flag them in the file and in your summary.'

risks_fired.evidence: tell me in 3-5 lines: the rules you wrote, which session moments grounded each one, and what you chose NOT to include and why.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Deliberate residual — body prose follow-up ('what did you choose NOT to write, and why?') is the designed counter. If hardening desired: 'Name the session moment that was messiest or most repeated — explain specifically why you did or did not write a rule from it.'

risks_fired.evidence: Review this session and write the first version of CLAUDE.md... Cover at minimum: the memory... the sources... the agents... and whatever else earned its place.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Deliberate residual — Debrief is a send-off; preamble is low-cost. If hardening desired: add sequencing directive inside fence: 'Write the file first. Then give the 3-5 line summary. No plan narration before writing.'
